ogTitle: Badjaling vs Bakers Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Badjaling and Bakers Hill.
heading: Badjaling vs Bakers Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Badjaling, Western Australia and Bakers Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Badjaling has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bakers Hill (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Badjaling or Bakers Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Badjaling has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Bakers Hill scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Badjaling scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Bakers Hill scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Badjaling or Bakers Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Badjaling has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
